title: T-028 — Rate-limit + observability wiring for maintenance Server Actions (Wave1 T-121, T-116..T-118)
prompt:
# T-028 — Rate-limit + observability for maintenance

Wave1 deps: T-121 (rate-limit primitive), T-116..T-118 (observability primitives — logger, tracing, metrics)
PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §6.4, §14.2 (RLS audit)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Wire all maintenance Server Actions (T-010 mwo-state, T-011 spare-parts, T-014 loto, T-015 calibration, T-016 sanitation, T-017 link-downtime) to Foundation rate-limit + observability primitives. Emit structured logs with `module='maintenance'`, span attrs with `mwo.id`/`spare.id`/`instrument.id`, and per-org rate limit buckets.

## Pre-flight
Verify Wave1 T-121 rate-limit primitive (`packages/rate-limit/src/index.ts` or similar) and Wave1 T-116..T-118 observability primitives (likely `packages/observability/src/{logger,tracer,metrics}.ts`).

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/with-observability.ts` — higher-order Server Action wrapper that:
   - Creates OpenTelemetry span `maintenance.<action_name>` with attrs `org_id`, `user_id`, `action`, and action-specific entity ids (mwo_id, spare_id, instrument_id).
   - Records structured log with `module='maintenance'`, `action`, duration, success/failure, error_code.
   - Increments counter `maintenance.actions.total{action, status}` and histogram `maintenance.actions.duration_ms`.
2. `apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/with-rate-limit.ts` — wrapper applying per-(org, user) rate limit buckets per Foundation T-121:
   - mwo write actions: 60/min/user.
   - calibration record: 30/min/user.
   - LOTO apply/clear: 10/min/user (safety: low rate).
   - spare consume/adjust: 120/min/user.
   - downtime link: 30/min/user.
3. Each existing Server Action from T-010/T-011/T-014/T-015/T-016/T-017 wrapped: `export const requestMwo = withObservability(withRateLimit({ bucket: 'mnt.mwo.write', limit: 60 }, _requestMwo));`.
4. Vitest covering: log includes module='maintenance' and action name; span attrs include entity id; rate limit returns 429 with `Retry-After` header after limit exceeded; histogram records duration.

## Files
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/with-observability.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/with-rate-limit.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/mwo-state.ts [modify — wrap actions]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/spare-parts.ts [modify]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/loto.ts [modify]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/calibration.ts [modify]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/sanitation.ts [modify]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_actions/link-downtime.ts [modify]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/__tests__/observability.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib/__tests__/rate-limit.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given any maintenance Server Action runs, when logs are inspected, then a structured log row with `module='maintenance'`, `action`, `duration_ms`, `status` is present and a span `maintenance.<action_name>` is started + ended.
2. Given a user calls requestMwo 61 times within 60 seconds, when 61st call settles, then it returns 429 with `Retry-After` header.
3. Given LOTO applyLoto called 11 times within 60 seconds, when 11th call settles, then 429 (low safety-bucket limit).
4. Given Prometheus counters are scraped, when checked, then `maintenance.actions.total{action='requestMwo',status='success'}` increments per successful call.
5. Given an action throws an error, when log emitted, then status='failure' + error_code captured.

## Test strategy
- RED: two vitest files asserting all five ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/app/maintenance/_lib`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not bypass Foundation rate-limit/observability primitives — they own the registry.
- Do not log raw PINs or PII — never include `verifierPin`, certifications full text, hourly_rate in span attrs or logs.
- Do not skip rate limit on safety-critical LOTO actions — safety bucket is mandatory.
- Do not introduce a parallel observability stack.
- Do not over-aggressively rate-limit reads (these wrappers cover writes only).

pipeline_inputs.description: Wire 6 Server Action modules through Foundation rate-limit (T-121) + observability (T-116..T-118) HoC wrappers; safety-bucket low limit on LOTO.
pipeline_inputs.details: PII-redaction in logs/spans is mandatory (no PINs, no certifications text, no hourly_rate). Safety bucket 10/min/user for LOTO actions. LOTO and calibration actions delegate PIN verification to @monopilot/e-sign foundation primitive (T-124) — signEvent/dualSign with replay nonce + paired audit (retention='security'); rate-limit wrappers must not intercept or log e-sign payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Given any maintenance Server Action runs, when logs are inspected, then a structured log row with module=maintenance, action, duration_ms, status is present and a span maintenance.<action_name> is started+ended

Given user calls requestMwo 61 times within 60 seconds, when 61st call settles, then returns 429 with Retry-After header; given LOTO applyLoto called 11 times within 60s, when 11th call settles, then 429 (low safety-bucket limit 10/min)
Given Prometheus counters scraped, when checked, then maintenance.actions.total{action=requestMwo,status=success} increments per successful call
Given an action throws an error, when log emitted, then status=failure + error_code captured; no raw PINs, certifications text, or hourly_rate in span attrs or logs
